PAF-T02-001.control_relationship: Primary adversarial case; paired with PAF-T02-003, which preserves the same typed cards and intake while neutralizing only procedure fields.
PAF-T02-001.diagnostic_objective: Detect whether favorable procedure metadata, lower incident identifier, or first candidate position can improperly influence representative selection despite a typed-selection discriminator.

PAF-T02-002.diagnostic_objective: Verify that reversing only candidate input order does not alter the primary adversarial selection result.

PAF-T02-003.control_relationship: Procedure-neutral control for PAF-T02-001; the non-procedure card fields and selection signatures are required to remain byte-for-byte equal after canonicalization.
PAF-T02-003.diagnostic_objective: Show whether the selection result remains stable when procedure list fields alone are neutralized, establishing that the primary case is not a trivial procedure mismatch.

PAV-001-procedure-asymmetric.purpose: Primary adversarial card set: one candidate has more favorable test-only procedure availability than the other while typed selection evidence remains unchanged.

PAV-002-procedure-neutral-control.purpose: Control card set: all non-procedure selection evidence is identical to PAV-001 while procedure lists are neutralized for both candidates.

non_claims: No selector or policy has been run against this proposal.
No selector or policy pass is claimed.
No active source incident card, procedure, policy, retrieval behavior, or production configuration is modified.
This proposal does not authorize selector activation or a Tranche 02 freeze.

permitted_selector_inputs: case-scoped HistoricalIncidentCard candidates from one family

v2_provenance_remediation.remediation_scope: PAV-001 / INC-013 now declares unsafe_procedure_ids as a controlled perturbation because source RB-002 removal isolates the test-only RB-003 procedure-availability asymmetry.
